title: Buzzing About Our Busy Bee Friends
story: Every May 20, people around the world celebrate World Bee Day by learning about these amazing little workers! Schools organize bee-themed activities where kids discover how bees make honey and help flowers grow. Families plant bee-friendly flowers like sunflowers and lavender in their gardens. Many people wear yellow and black stripes to honor their favorite pollinators. Beekeepers open their hives for special tours, showing visitors how thousands of bees work together like a tiny city. Libraries host story times about busy bees, while nature centers teach kids the waggle dance that bees use to communicate. Some families even enjoy honey-sweetened treats to celebrate these incredible insects that help grow our food!
activity_question: What dance do bees use to communicate with each other?
bonus_fact: A single bee visits about 2,000 flowers every day to collect nectar!

title: Measuring Up on National Weights Day
story: Rulers, scales, and measuring cups get their moment to shine on May 20 during National Weights and Measures Day! People celebrate by appreciating all the tools that help us measure everything from ingredients for cookies to how tall we've grown. Some folks even share fun measuring facts online or try measuring weird things around their house.
activity_question: What tools do people appreciate on National Weights and Measures Day?
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest thing in your house you could try measuring today?
